oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Tips for finding your “Go Go Gadget” arms for OB cases
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Wiley, Caitlin
oai_dc:dc.dc:subject: dystocia
OB chains
short-stature
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: As a cattle veterinarian, you are faced with daily opportunities to be resourceful, adaptable and think on your feet to get the task at hand done. Each farm you walk onto has its own, often unique, working facilities that you learn to work with. Layer on the char­acteristics of the cattle being raised, the producer running the operation, and your own physique, you begin to figure out how to ultimately turn into “Inspector Gadget”. For folks who are short­er-statured, working cattle can be a true challenge and frustra­tion, especially when it involves a reproductive related call. This Clinical Skills session will cover some tips and suggestions to hopefully help ease the anxiety and frustrations of reproductive-related calls as a newer veterinarian and as a height-restricted person. It will discuss educating your clients and utilizing the tools and resources around you to your advantage.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: American Association of Bovine Practitioners Conference Proceedings; No. 1 (2026): 2026 Recent Graduate Conference Proceedings; 62-64
oai_dc:dc.dc:rights: Copyright (c) 2026 American Association of Bovine Practitioners
